task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open showing the Aerobridge Django admin dashboard or login page at http://localhost:8000/admin/.

task_completion.description: Select the 'Draft Survey Mission' flight plan for editing
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser shows the edit page for the 'Draft Survey Mission' under the Flight Plans section in the Aerobridge admin.
The 'Start time' input field on the flight plan edit page contains the value '2025-11-15 08:30:00'.
The 'End time' input field on the flight plan edit page contains the value '2025-11-15 12:45:00'.

task_completion.description: Update the Geometry field with valid GeoJSON
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Geometry' input field contains a valid GeoJSON object with '"type": "Polygon"' and coordinates matching '[[[-95.1, 38.9], [-95.09, 38.9], [-95.09, 38.91], [-95.1, 38.91], [-95.1, 38.9]]]' (Longitude, Latitude order).
A success message (e.g., 'The flight plan "Draft Survey Mission" was changed successfully.') is visible in the admin interface, or the browser has returned to the flight plan list after saving.

integrity.description: The agent used the web browser to interact with the Aerobridge admin interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser being used to navigate and fill out forms, rather than terminal commands (like curl or python scripts) making API requests.

integrity.description: The agent correctly formatted the coordinates into a GeoJSON Polygon.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Geometry field contains properly formatted JSON with 'type' and 'coordinates' keys, not just a raw copy-paste of the text file contents.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Aerobridge application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows 'localhost:8000' or '127.0.0.1:8000', confirming interaction with the live application and not a local HTML file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target flight plan: 'Draft Survey Mission'. Expected Start Time: '2025-11-15 08:30:00'. Expected End Time: '2025-11-15 12:45:00'. Expected Geometry: A GeoJSON object with type 'Polygon' and coordinates [[[-95.1, 38.9], [-95.09, 38.9], [-95.09, 38.91], [-95.1, 38.91], [-95.1, 38.9]]] (Note: GeoJSON requires Longitude first, then Latitude, and the polygon must be closed by repeating the first coordinate at the end).
